growth_records.medium: Anaerobic batch medium with lactate and glucose as main electron donors (composition not stated in main text)

up.method: reported qualitatively; lactate utilized later and more slowly than M. elsdenii; grows slowly on lactate; may have higher affinity (lower Ks) for lactate (Supplementary Fig. S3B); numeric time-course only in Fig. 4B
reported: 'no consumption of glucose was observed' for M. hexanoica (Fig. 4B); glucose not consumed
reported qualitatively; acetate co-substrate of chain elongation (Fig. 4B)

sec.method: reported qualitatively; C4 via extensive reverse beta-oxidation/chain elongation from lactate (Fig. 4B, Fig. 6B)
reported qualitatively; medium-chain fatty acid from lactate-driven chain elongation (Fig. 4B)
reported qualitatively; MCFA produced via chain elongation (Fig. 4B, C2-C6,C8 shown)
reported qualitatively; only small amounts produced (M. hexanoica lacks the acrylate pathway), possibly from amino acid catabolism (Fig. 6B)

yields.ATP_yield_rBOX_chain_elongation: 0.5 mol ATP per mol lactate (Enzyme Cost Minimization model prediction for the reverse beta-oxidation/chain elongation pathway, which M. hexanoica runs); enzyme cost per ATP flux 4-7 fold higher than the acrylate pathway

growth_records.cite: Strachan et al. Distinct lactate utilization strategies drive niche differentiation between two co-existing Megasphaera species in the rumen microbiome. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf147. [PMC12342382]
growth_records.snippet: For M. hexanoica, no consumption of glucose was observed, and lactate was utilized later and more slowly ... M. hexanoica carries out extensive chain elongation and grows slowly on lactate compared to M. elsdenii ... rBOX yields 0.5 [mole A
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic lactate+glucose experiment (Fig. 4B). Explicit qualitative fact: glucose NOT consumed (qGlucose = 0). No numeric growth rate, doubling time, qS or qP in main-text XML; growth/substrate/VFA (C2-C6,C8) curves only in Fig. 4B/Fig. 6B and max specific growth rate + Ks in Supplementary Fig. S3B (not in this file). Temperature/pH not stated for this experiment (lactate-only experiment used starting pH 5.5). ATP-per-lactate yield 0.5 is an ECM model prediction (k+=200 1/s, Km=0.2 mM assumed), not a measured yield; enzyme cost per ATP flux for rBOX is 4-7x the acrylate pathway. M. hexanoica grows slower and is predicted to have lower max growth rate but higher ATP yield than M. elsdenii; inferred replication rate ~1.5-fold lower (Supplementary Table S2). No arithmetic derivable from main text.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 140555 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/140555
